The **Eternal derby** ([Romanian](/wiki/Romanian_language "Romanian language"): *Eternul derby*), also called the **Romanian derby** ([Romanian](/wiki/Romanian_language "Romanian language"): *Derby-ul României* or *Derby de România*) or the **Great derby** ([Romanian](/wiki/Romanian_language "Romanian language"): *Marele derby*), is a [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") match between [Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest") rivals *[FCSB](/wiki/FCSB "FCSB")* and *[Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București")*. The game is usually among—if not the most—viewed and attended of the [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") season.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The two most successful clubs in [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"),[[note 1]](#cite_note-Steaua-4) they won a combined 95 honours: a record 61 for Steaua/FCSB (including two international trophies) and 34 for Dinamo București.

The teams have played 191 matches in all domestic top-level competitions. FCSB have won 72 times to Dinamo's 62, with 57 drawn.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eternal_derby_(Romania)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The first game between the two teams was played on 21 November 1948. Dinamo won 1–0. The events are regularly marred by instances of hooliganism and frequent outbreaks of violence between rival fan groups.[[4]](#cite_note-5)[[5]](#cite_note-6)[[6]](#cite_note-7)[[7]](#cite_note-8)

The two teams are notable for their collective dominance of Romanian football.[[note 1]](#cite_note-Steaua-4) Between them, FCSB (27 times) and Dinamo (18 times) have won the [Romanian football championship](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") 45 times out of 106 completed seasons, resulting in a percentage of 42.45%. This supremacy extends also to the [Cupa României](/wiki/Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "Cupa României") (24 times for FCSB and 13 times for Dinamo), to the [Supercupa României](/wiki/Supercupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "Supercupa României") (7 times for FCSB and 2 times for Dinamo) and to the [Cupa Ligii](/wiki/Cupa_Ligii "Cupa Ligii") (2 times for FCSB and 1 time for Dinamo).

They are also the two Romanian football teams with the best results in club competitions organised by [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA").[[note 1]](#cite_note-Steaua-4) FCSB won the [European Cup](/wiki/European_Cup "European Cup") in [1986](/wiki/European_Cup_1985%E2%80%9386 "European Cup 1985–86"), was runner-up in the same competition in [1989](/wiki/European_Cup_1988%E2%80%9389 "European Cup 1988–89") after reaching the semi-finals in [1988](/wiki/European_Cup_1987%E2%80%9388 "European Cup 1987–88"). In [2006](/wiki/UEFA_Cup_2005%E2%80%9306 "UEFA Cup 2005–06"), FCSB reached the semi-finals of [UEFA Europa League](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League"). Meanwhile, Dinamo reached the semi-finals of the European Cup in [1984](/wiki/European_Cup_1983%E2%80%9384 "European Cup 1983–84") and in the [Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/Cup_Winners%27_Cup "Cup Winners' Cup") in [1990](/wiki/UEFA_Cup_Winners%27_Cup_1989%E2%80%9390 "UEFA Cup Winners' Cup 1989–90"). Additionally, FCSB won the [UEFA Super Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Super_Cup "UEFA Super Cup") in [1986](/wiki/1986_UEFA_Super_Cup "1986 UEFA Super Cup"), but was defeated in the [Intercontinental Cup](/wiki/Intercontinental_Cup_(1960%E2%80%932004) "Intercontinental Cup (1960–2004)") by [River Plate](/wiki/River_Plate_F.C. "River Plate F.C.") the same year.

The rivalry also extends to a number of other sports, including [rugby](/wiki/Rugby_football "Rugby football"), [handball](/wiki/Team_handball "Team handball") and [water polo](/wiki/Water_polo "Water polo").
